prompt: Fix the multipart form parser that corrupts binary file uploads

turns.content:
<task>
Fix the multipart form parser — binary file uploads are corrupted because the parser decodes content as UTF-8.
</task>

1	from typing import Dict, Tuple
   2	
   3	class FormParser:
   4	    def parse(self, body: bytes, boundary: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
   5	        result = {}
   6	        parts = body.decode('utf-8').split(f'--{boundary}')
   7	        for part in parts:
   8	            part = part.strip()
   9	            if not part or part == '--':
  10	                continue
  11	            if '\r\n\r\n' in part:
  12	                header, content = part.split('\r\n\r\n', 1)
  13	                name = self._extract_name(header)
  14	                if name:
  15	                    result[name] = content
  16	        return result
  17	
  18	    def _extract_name(self, header: str) -> str:
  19	        import re
  20	        match = re.search(r'name="([^"]+)"', header)
  21	        return match.group(1) if match else ''

arguments.reasoning: Line 6: `body.decode('utf-8')` corrupts binary data. The parser should work with bytes and only decode text fields. For binary files, content should remain as bytes. Need to split on bytes boundary and return a Union[str, bytes] dict.

arguments.summary: Fixed binary corruption in formparser/parser.py. Was decoding entire body as UTF-8. Now works with raw bytes: splits on boundary bytes, decodes headers only, checks 'filename=' to determine if content is file (keep as bytes) or text (decode). Found file via glob('**/form*') after '**/multipart*' had no matches. All 4 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed binary file upload corruption in formparser/parser.py. Parser now works with bytes throughout, only decoding text fields and headers. Binary file content preserved as bytes. All 4 tests pass.
